summary: Frontend-only end-to-end verification of the Next.js 16 portfolio (Dylan Garnier). Tested public page loads, login screen (password hint removed), admin login, persistence bug fix (admin edits → public site), Tableau E5 editor persistence, and Bloc-1 detailed competences (code + intitulé + Argument + Preuve) on a réalisation detail page. ALL 8 assertions PASSED.
critical_code_review_comments: app/admin/page.tsx is very large (>1600 lines) and mixes data, dialogs and several tabs. Recommend splitting into per-tab components (ProjectsTab, SkillsTab, VeilleTab, E5Tab, ProfileTab) and extracting the persist() / saveStatus logic into a useContentStore hook to improve maintainability.

Admin auth is purely client-side (sessionStorage + hard-coded ADMIN_PASSWORD in lib/auth-context.tsx). The /api/content PUT endpoint is unauthenticated, so anyone who can reach the deployment can overwrite the published content via curl. Consider gating PUT with a server-side check (e.g., an env-based bearer token or NextAuth).

handleAddProject mutates state then immediately calls persist({ projects: [newProjectData, ...projects] }) using the pre-update projects array — the persisted payload is correct only because it is reconstructed locally; this is fragile. Consider passing the freshly computed array from a single source of truth.
saveE5 saves the current realisationsE5 state but does not echo a confirmation specific to E5 — fine functionally, just noting the UX shares the global saveStatus banner.
Login form (components/admin/login-form.tsx) confirmed: no occurrence of 'btssio2024' or password hint.
test_credentials: Admin password: btssio2024 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Used to log in via /admin password field.
seed_data_creation: Created and then removed a transient marker 'MARKER_TEST_123' (project description) and 'E5_MARKER_456' (Tableau E5 first textarea). The dev fallback file /app/.data/portfolio-content.json was deleted at the end of testing to leave a clean state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All testing must target http://localhost:3000 directly, NOT the external preview URL — the Kubernetes ingress reserves '/api' for a different backend that is stopped, so /api/content is only reachable via localhost where Next.js serves it. Dev persistence uses /app/.data/portfolio-content.json (gitignored). /api/upload, /api/gallery, /api/files require BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN and are not testable in dev — out of scope. Admin password lives in lib/auth-context.tsx (ADMIN_PASSWORD = 'btssio2024'). data-testid attributes verified present: save-status, save-e5-btn, add-comp-detail-btn.
